message.example: Cannot unlink: no password set. Please set a password first.

get.description: Returns all connections (active + recent) with backward-compatible single-bridge fields.
get.summary: Get the current team's bridge connection status

post.description:
The customer pastes their tunnel URL (Cloudflare Tunnel, Tailscale Funnel, ngrok, etc.).
FleetQ calls /discover to validate and fetch the available agents/LLMs.
post.summary: Connect a bridge via HTTP tunnel URL (HTTP mode)

put.description: Use when the tunnel URL changes (e.g., Cloudflare quick tunnels regenerate on restart).
put.summary: Update the endpoint URL for an HTTP-mode bridge connection

post.description: Calls GET /health on the bridge server and updates last_seen_at / status.
post.summary: Ping an HTTP-mode bridge connection to verify it is reachable

error.const: Ping is only available for HTTP-mode connections.

post.summary: Register or refresh a bridge connection (called by the bridge daemon on connect)
Update the endpoints manifest (called by the bridge daemon on discovery)
Heartbeat — keeps the connection alive (called by the bridge daemon periodically)

post.description:
Uses the same Redis frame-based protocol as all bridge communication:
RPUSH to bridge:req:{teamId} → relay binary → WebSocket → bridge daemon → MCP server.
post.summary: Proxy an MCP tool call through the relay to a bridge-hosted MCP server

delete.description:
Accepts optional session_id (relay-generated, used to guard against reconnect races)
or connection_id to disconnect a specific bridge.
Without either, disconnects all active bridges for the team.

post.description:
Generates the HMAC-SHA256 auth signature manually instead of using
Broadcast::auth() which has guard resolution issues in API context.
post.summary: Authenticate a Pusher/Reverb private channel subscription

Returns merged config: database overrides take precedence over env values.
API keys are never returned — only whether they are set.

In cloud deployments only super admins may call this endpoint.

get.description:
Returns every host:port combination that has been trusted via TOFU
(Trust On First Use) for the current team.
get.summary: List all trusted SSH host fingerprints

delete.description:
Removes the stored fingerprint for this host:port. The next SSH connection
to that host will re-run TOFU and store a fresh fingerprint.
Use this when a host's SSH key has been rotated legitimately.
delete.summary: Delete a trusted SSH host fingerprint
